Title: Recent advances in molecular farming using monocot plants

Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Upregulation mitigates callus browning and improves genetic transformation efficiency in rice.
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. BOC1 was reported as responsible for mitigating callus browning in rice. 2. Upregulation consequently improved genetic transformation efficiency. 3. The review highlights it as a manipulable factor to create recipient cereal varieties more amenable to transformation.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful target for improving cereal transformation amenability rather than nutrient metabolism directly.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Included because it is one of few named host genes emphasized in the review, but it is not a nutrient-metabolism gene; most named genes in this review are promoters/storage proteins or transformation factors rather than metabolism genes.

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Seed lipid storage; used here as fusion carrier for recombinant protein recovery
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Fusion to oleosin enables targeting of recombinant proteins to oil-body surfaces and simplifies purification while potentially avoiding ER glycosylation.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Summarized prior transgenic seed fusion-expression studies
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Oleosin fusion directs heterologous proteins to oil-body surfaces. 2. Oil bodies can be isolated by flotation centrifugation for simple recovery. 3. Fusion to oleosin may help avoid post-translational modifications in the ER lumen.
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Fusion tag may require removal; may alter bioactivity of target protein.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Valuable as a fusion partner for low-cost purification platforms in cereal seeds.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Classified as Common Gene because it is a structural/storage targeting protein rather than enzyme or regulator.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Condenses two geranylgeranyl diphosphate molecules to form phytoene.
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Often key control point, but not established by this review directly
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Forms immediate precursor pool for downstream carotenoids
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Nutritional provitamin A and antioxidant functions depending on downstream products
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Used as a heterologous enzyme expressed in wheat grains in molecular farming examples.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Prior transgenic expression study cited in Table 2
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Phytoene synthase is listed as a recombinant product expressed in wheat seed under the 1Dx5 promoter. 2. This demonstrates cereal endosperm suitability for expressing a carotenoid pathway enzyme. 3. No direct nutrient phenotype in host grain is detailed in this review.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential enzyme for carotenoid biofortification stacks, though this review discusses it only as recombinant product expression.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Named in review table as recombinant product; not a central metabolism-discovery gene of the review.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Introduces double bonds into carotene intermediates during carotenoid biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Antioxidant/pigment and nutritional functions depending on downstream carotenoids
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Listed as a recombinant product expressed in maize using barley Hor3-1 promoter.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Prior transgenic expression study cited in Table 2
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Carotene desaturase was expressed in maize endosperm in a cited study. 2. The example supports cross-species promoter use in cereal molecular farming. 3. No direct host nutrient phenotype is detailed in this review.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential component for carotenoid pathway engineering, although review emphasis is recombinant enzyme production rather than metabolic trait analysis.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Named only as an example recombinant product in the review.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Transfers fucose in α1,3-linkage to the glycan core of plant N-glycans.
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key determinant of plant-specific glycan structure
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Required for plant-specific glycosylation pattern rather than nutrient accumulation
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Inactivation removes undesirable plant-specific α1,3-fucose from recombinant glycoproteins in rice biopharming platforms.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Review summary of multiplex CRISPR/Cas9 inactivation study
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Endogenous α1,3-fucosyltransferase was identified as a source of plant-specific N-glycan modification. 2. Its inactivation was used to humanize rice glycosylation for biopharmaceutical production. 3. The review frames it as a bottleneck-removal target for molecular farming.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Potential effects on endogenous glycoprotein processing not detailed.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Important target for generating human-compatible glycosylation in cereal-based bioproduction systems.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Not a nutrient metabolism gene in classical sense; included due to explicit causal role in product glycoform engineering.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Transfers xylose in β1,2-linkage to the glycan core of plant N-glycans.
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key determinant of plant-specific glycan structure
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Required for plant-specific glycosylation pattern rather than nutrient accumulation
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Inactivation removes undesirable plant-specific β1,2-xylose from recombinant glycoproteins in rice biopharming platforms.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Review summary of multiplex CRISPR/Cas9 inactivation study
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Endogenous β1,2-xylosyltransferase generates plant-specific N-glycan structures. 2. CRISPR/Cas9 inactivation in rice was highlighted to improve biopharmaceutical compatibility. 3. The review presents it as a key glycoengineering target.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Potential effects on endogenous glycoprotein processing not detailed.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Important target for humanized glycosylation in cereal molecular farming.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Not a nutrient metabolism gene in classical nutritional-trait sense; included because it is a core enzymatic glycoengineering target explicitly discussed.

Regulation_Genes.Applied_Species_Latin_Name: Zea mays; Sorghum bicolor; Oryza sativa; Saccharum spp.
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: AP2/ERF morphogenic transcription factor Baby boom
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Transcriptional developmental reprogramming to induce embryogenic callus formation
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Overexpression induced recovery of transgenic calli and boosted transformation frequencies in multiple monocots.
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Overexpression of maize Bbm improved transformation recovery in monocots. 2. Transformation frequency increased strongly compared with controls. 3. The review cites Bbm as a morphogenic factor for overcoming transformation recalcitrance.
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Key factor for transformability, not direct nutrient-product regulation.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Valuable for improving transformation efficiency in cereal engineering pipelines.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Not nutrient metabolism specific; included as one of the few explicit regulatory genes discussed.

Regulation_Genes.Applied_Species_Latin_Name: Zea mays; Sorghum bicolor; Oryza sativa; Saccharum spp.
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Transcriptional developmental reprogramming to induce transgenic callus recovery
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Overexpression promoted transgenic callus recovery and increased transformation frequencies in several monocots.
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Wus2 is highlighted with Bbm as improving monocot transformation. 2. Presence of Wus2 and Bbm raised transformation frequencies dramatically. 3. It is relevant to engineering pipeline efficiency rather than nutrient metabolism directly.
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Key factor for transformability, not direct nutrient-product regulation.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful enabling regulator for monocot transformation platforms.
